title: Deuteronomy: Heart Obedience
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of deuteronomy: heart obedience and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study deuteronomy: heart obedience and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What warning did Moses give Israel about wealth in the promised land in Deuteronomy 8? Answer: do not say in your heart that your own power produced your wealth; remember the LORD who led you through the wilderness.
segments.correct_answer: do not forget the LORD by thinking your own strength produced your wealth
acceptable_variants: beware of forgetting god when you are prosperous
do not say my power built this wealth — remember the LORD
prosperity without remembrance of God leads to destruction

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Moses recalls Israel's forty years in the wilderness in Deuteronomy 8 as God's humbling and testing, teaching that man does not live by bread alone but by every word of God

vocabulary.term: land flowing with milk and honey
vocabulary.definition: The recurring description of Canaan's abundance in Deuteronomy; it signifies God's generous provision for his people but also carries the warning that provision can become a source of pride
